channel.title: Arch Linux on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Arch Linux on Memo

item.title: Install Arch Linux (BTRFS + Timeshift)
item.description: Install Arch Linux (BTRFS + Timeshift) # Installation manual for Arch Linux. This article describes how to install Arch Linux on BTRFS filesystem and save snapshots with Timeshift. System # Intel CPU UEFI Boot Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: Install Arch Linux (Multi-disks + BTRFS + Snapper)
item.description: Install Arch Linux (Multi-disks + BTRFS + Snapper) # Installation manual for Arch Linux. This article describes how to install Arch Linux on BTRFS filesystem and save snapshots with Snapper. System # Intel CPU UEFI Boot Disks /dev/vda /dev/vdb /dev/vdc Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: Dual Booting (mono disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux)
item.description: Dual Booting (mono disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux) # System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel Storage: /dev/sda Preparation in Windows 10 # First, install Windows 10, then run Disk Management to create a free space for the Linux installation. The layout before and after creating the free partition will look like this. Before: After: Then, install Arch Linux as follows.

item.title: Dual Booting (multi-disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux)
item.description: Dual Booting (multi-disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux) # System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel GPU: Nvidia Storage: /dev/sda: Windows sub /dev/sdb: Windows main /dev/sdc: Linux Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: Desktop Environment
item.description: Desktop Environment # This is a tutorial for installing desktop environment. Install # Graphics Driver # Install graphics driver. Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Nvidia sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Display Server # Install xorg as a display server. A display server is software that passes input and output using a GUI such as Gnome to the OS and hardware.

item.title: Installing BSPWM
item.description: Installing BSPWM # Install and customize a Tile-type window manager called BSPWM. Install and Configurations # Graphics Driver # Install graphics driver. Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Nvidia sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms BSPWM # Install display server, bspwm and etc. sudo pacman -S xorg xorg-xinit bspwm picom dmenu sxhkd nitrogen arandr Install a terminal emulator and a browser.

item.title: Install Applications
item.description: Install Applications # Package Manager # Paru git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/paru cd paru makepkg -si Yay git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git cd yay makepkg -si Shell # zsh sudo pacman -S zsh zsh-completions chsh -s $(which zsh) # or chsh -s /bin/zsh Terminal Emulator # Alacritty sudo pacman -S alacritty Xfce4 terminal sudo pacman -S xfce4-terminal Gnome terminal paru -S gnome-terminal-transparency Terminal multiplexer # tmux sudo pacman -S tmux Editor # VSCode paru -S visual-studio-code-bin vim sudo pacman -S gvim emacs sudo pacman -S emacs-nox Backup # Timeshift paru -S timeshift Browser # Brave browser paru -S brave-bin Virtual Machine Monitor # KVM

item.title: Power Management
item.description: Power Management # This is a memo about power management. TLP # TLP is a power management tool for Linux. Once installed and activated, it can be used without any special configuration. Install # sudo pacman -S tlp Settings # If you are using BTRFS as a file system, you should make the following settings because the file system may be corrupted. sudo vim /etc/default/tlp SATA_LINKPWR_ON_BAT=max_performance Enable TLP # Enable TLP by running following commands.

item.title: Arch Build System
item.description: Arch Build System # ABS is a system that automates the building of packages from source code; it uses makepkg to build packages based on information in PKGBUILD, and pacman to install them. Install # Install asp to use ABS. sudo pacman -S asp Usage (kernel build) # The following is an example of how to build a kernel. Create build/ as a working directory. mkdir build cd build Download the PKGBUILD for the Linux kernel.

item.title: Configurations for Spotify
item.description: Configurations for Spotify # This is a page about Spotify settings and troubleshooting. HiDPI # If you use a high resolution monitor, the default UI is too small, so change the settings to make it easier to see. By modifying /usr/share/applications/spotify.desktop as follows, you can change the scale of Spotify when it is launched in the GUI. -Exec=spotify %U +Exec=spotify --force-device-scale-factor=1.5 %U Download Error # Since Spotify has a lot of download errors, I’ll write down some of the errors and solutions below.
